channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Thousands left without power in Melbourne CBD after local outage
item.description: A power company was forced to switch off power to about 10,000 customers in the heart of Melbourne after a burst water main threatened to flood a substation.

media:group.media:description: A burst water main has caused a power outage in Melbourne's CBD.

item.title: 'Stop One Nation': Labor launches campaign to target ONP rise
item.description: Federal Labor is asking for donations to fight One Nation as the latest polling shows the minor party continuing to surge in key electorates, including in Western Sydney.
item.dc:creator: Jason Om

media:group.media:description: New polling has found One Nation could win enough seats to become the opposition if a federal election was held now.

item.title: Blues send out SOS as playmaker struggles with injury
item.description: A hamstring complaint for Mitchell Moses puts a scare through the NSW Blues, with Ethan Strange stepping up and Matt Burton called into camp ahead of State of Origin I.

media:group.media:description: Mitchell Moses will get scans on his hamstring tomorrow.

item.title: Victoria will not introduce firearm caps or buyback scheme, following review
item.description: There will be no limits introduced to the number of guns a person can own in Victoria, following a rapid review into the state's gun laws.
item.dc:creator: Keira Proust and Judd Boaz

media:group.media:description: Victoria will adopt all but one of the recommendations made by former police chief Ken Lay in his rapid review of the state's gun laws.

item.title: NACC boss Paul Brereton resigns amid pressure over misconduct, defence ties
item.description: Mr Brereton was found in 2024 to have engaged in officer misconduct in his role as head of the NACC and has since faced pressure over his defence connections.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas McElroy

media:group.media:description: Paul Brereton is still expected to face Senate budget estimates following his announcement.

item.title: 'Fool, ignoramus': Parliamentary committee suspended as senators clash
item.description: A heated exchange prompted a Liberal senator to step in and call point of order on the Labor chair after several insults directed at Nationals senator Bridget McKenzie, before a brief suspension.

media:group.media:description: Bridget McKenzie, David Littleproud, Kevin Hogan announce the federal Nationals are leaving the coalition.

item.title: Renewables company destroys Indigenous shelter despite knowing about it
item.description: ACREZ apologises to traditional owners "without reservation" as the NSW Environment Minister Penny Sharpe expresses her "fury" at the incident.
item.dc:creator: Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: The Central West Orana Renewable Energy Zone spans 20,000 square kilometres within inland NSW.

item.title: Jury to visit location of Frances Crawford's alleged murder, court hears
item.description: Robert Crawford is charged with the murder of his wife Frances Crawford, 49, who was found dead partially trapped underneath a ride-on lawn mower at their Upper Lockyer property in July, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: Frances Crawford was found dead at her Upper Lockyer home, partially trapped underneath a ride-on lawnmower.

item.title: ISIS-linked women and children to return to Australia within days
item.description: A group of women and children linked to Islamic State fighters are expected to arrive home within days after purchasing flights to Australia.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould and Sara Tomevska

media:group.media:description: A group has left the al-Roj refugee camp.

item.title: Should bore water be sold as spring water?
item.description: A resident of the Perth Hills, where thousands of litres of groundwater are extracted each week and sold as bottled water, has questioned whether it's accurate to label it as 'spring water'.

media:group.media:description: Many bottled water brands use 'spring water' labelling, which is allowed under the Australian Food Standards Code if the water is groundwater.

item.title: Victim of fatal Queensland shark attack identified
item.description: While the Far North Queensland community is reeling from the death of local spearfisher Michael Jensz, experts warn human-shark interactions are increasing.
item.dc:creator: Holly Richardson, Charlie McKillop, Rosanne Maloney, and Conor Byrne

media:group.media:description: Emergency services were called to the Hull River boat ramp.

item.title: Man, 23, charged with murder over death of eight-week-old baby
item.description: A 23-year-old man has been charged with murder over the death of an eight-week-old child in suburban Perth.

media:group.media:description: Police tape tied to a police car blocking access to the Murray Street Mall in Perth after a bomb scare at an Invasion Day rally on 26/1/26.

item.title: Committee rejects calls to review greyhound phase-out bill
item.description: A parliamentary committee has rejected a request to review a bill which would phase out Tasmania's greyhound racing industry. It follows new data which shows 19 ex-racing greyhounds have died in Tasmania this year.
item.dc:creator: Clancy Balen and Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: The fate of the state's greyhound industry rests on a vote in the upper house of parliament, but the timeline remains unclear on when this will happen.

item.title: Neale Daniher to be remembered with state funeral, premier reveals
item.description: A great of the game and icon after his retirement, Neale Daniher is being remembered as a fighter and an inspiration after his death.

media:group.media:description: Neale Daniher will be honoured with a state funeral.

item.title: Notorious Perth paedophile Bradley Pen Dragon released from jail again
item.description: A notorious Perth paedophile who breached his prison release conditions 11 times has again been freed into the community, as the child protection minister argues it may be unconstitutional to keep him in custody.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Goerling

media:group.media:description: Dragon served a 13-year jail term in Thailand for sexually assaulting a teenage girl.

item.title: V'landys to take over as NRL CEO after Abdo quits to join Tennis Australia
item.description: NRL CEO Andrew Abdo causes a shock in the rugby league world by departing mid-season after a highly-successful six-year stint.

media:group.media:description: Andrew Abdo leaves the NRL after a highly-successful six-year tenure as the league's CEO.

item.title: Dezi Freeman goaded officers to 'come and get me' before fatal shooting
item.description: The Coroners Court has heard about Dezi Freeman's final moments before a fatal confrontation at his hideout in Victoria's north-east.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Dezi Freeman was on the run for seven months before he was shot by police earlier this year.

item.title: Breaking: Neale Daniher, former AFL player and motor neurone disease campaigner, dies
item.description: Former Essendon player and Demons coach Neale Daniher has died, aged 65. He was diagnosed with motor neurone disease in 2013 and campaigned to raise awareness of the disease and increase research.
item.dc:creator: Tim Callanan

media:group.media:description: Daniher established the Big Freeze fundraising event for Motor Neurone Disease, which has run for a number of years.

item.title: Budget-friendly mince dishes that will feed the family
item.description: Mix up your mince menu with these versatile and affordable meals for the whole family.
item.dc:creator: Amy Sheehan

media:group.media:description: Set up this slow-cooker bolognese the night before you plan to eat it and look forward to it all day.

item.title: Government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to regional airports
item.description: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.
item.dc:creator: Jenae Madden and Lily Plass

media:group.media:description: Thirty-four councils across Australia have received part of a $4.8 million bailout, including Dubbo Regional Council which was owed $598,672.

item.title: Dog-friendly cafe issued warning after customer complaint
item.description: Kellie Hunter opened her cafe in the Adelaide Hills in 2023 as a friendly place for dogs and their owners, but the local council has now put a stop to it after one complaint.
item.dc:creator: Eugene Boisvert and Bobby Macumber

media:group.media:description: Same But Different Cafe owner Kellie Hunter can now only serve "puppacinos" outside.

item.title: Why it's about to get so much harder to find an aged care bed in WA
item.description: It's going to get much harder to find an available aged care bed in WA, a new report finds, with a "substantial and time-critical shortfall" predicted.
item.dc:creator: Keane Bourke

media:group.media:description: WA is facing a "time-critical" aged care bed shortage by 2030.

item.title: Mobile GP service and after-hours care to end because of Tasmanian health cuts
item.description: Primary care providers being forced to cut services after losing Tasmanian government funding say the result will be added strain on "pressure cooker" emergency departments, which could see them "explode".
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: An after-hours clinic in Cygnet will end after its funding was cut in last week's budget.

item.title: Aboriginal community workers say diphtheria response 'heartbreaking'
item.description: Residents and service providers in the remote community of Yuendumu, where there are reports of dozens of diphtheria cases, say the NT government has not done enough to keep people safe.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange

media:group.media:description: Yuendumu residents have told the ABC there have been about 30 confirmed cases of diphtheria in the Central Australian community.

item.title: 'I did it': Parramatta council official fronts corruption watchdog
item.description: During her third day on the witness stand, Roxanne Thornton told the Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) she was responsible for hiring processes that were "extremely flawed".
item.dc:creator: Nabil Al-Nashar

media:group.media:description: Roxanne Thornton told ICAC she edited job applications for friends to help them secure jobs with City of Parramatta Council.
